data.title: Capricor Therapeutics prices $150M stock offering
data.description:
• Capricor Therapeutics (CAPR) shares popped over 4% premarket on Friday after the biotechnology company priced an offering to raise $150M.
  • The offering includes 6M shares of common stock issued at a public offering price of $25.00 per share. Capricor also granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 900K shares.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for the continued development and manufacturing of its product candidates, for working capital, and for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 8, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • CAPR +4.8% premarket to $26.63
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Airbus faces steep December push as technical snags threaten delivery goal
data.description:
Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) faces a steep climb to close out the year, needing to hand over 133 aircraft in December to meet its updated annual delivery goal. The challenge comes as the manufacturer deals with an unusual cluster of technical issues that could pull attention away from production.

The company reported Friday that it delivered 72 jets in November, bringing the year-to-date total to 657. Hitting the target is still possible, as Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) managed 123 deliveries last December alone, but this year’s final stretch is being complicated by quality concerns tied to the A320 family.

The past several days have underscored the pressure. On Wednesday, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) cut its delivery guidance by 30 aircraft. Days earlier, airlines worldwide were forced to rush through a software patch affecting more than 6,000 A320-series jets after engineers discovered that solar radiation could interfere with flight-control systems. And on Monday, the company disclosed that it must examine several hundred A320s for potentially faulty fuselage panels.

Despite the turbulence, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) continued to build its order book. In November it logged 75 new gross orders, including a fresh commitment from IndiGo for 30 A350-900 widebodies, lifting the Indian carrier’s Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) backlog to 919 aircraft.

data.title: Putin: If U.S. has the right to buy fuel from us, why shouldn't India?
data.description:
Russia's President Vladimir Putin asserted that India should enjoy the same privilege as the U.S. for buying Russian fuel, according to an interview with India Today that aired hours after he landed in New Delhi.

"The U.S. continues to buy nuclear fuel from us for their nuclear power plants," Putin pointed out. "That is also fuel. Energy. This is uranium for nuclear power plants that are functioning in the U.S."

"If the U.S. has the right to purchase fuel from us, why should India be deprived of such a right? That is a matter to be studied closely, and we are prepared to discuss that and debate that with President Trump," he added.

Russia currently supplies around 20%-30% of enriched uranium product used in the U.S. and Europe, according to the Department of Energy. The U.S. will completely ban all uranium imports from Russia by 2028.

The Trump administration imposed punitive tariffs on Indian goods because of India's ongoing purchases of Russian oil. Since then, India has scaled back these imports.

The Russian leader arrived in India on Thursday for a two-day state visit, his first trip to the country in four years. 

Putin and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi agreed to expand economic cooperation across sectors from defense to shipping, and accelerate progress toward the $100B bilateral trade target by 2030.

Putin also said Russia was "ready to continue uninterrupted fuel shipments" to India.

data.title: The World Cup is coming in 2026: How investors can get the ball rolling
data.description:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be the largest World Cup ever, with 48 teams playing 104 matches across 16 host cities in the United States, Canada, and Mexico from June 11 to July 19, 2026. The focus on the tournament will get a boost on December 5 when the draw is announced at 12:00 p.m. in Washington, D.C., setting all the group-stage matchups and each team’s path through the expanded knockout rounds.

It is very likely that the 2026 World Cup will be the most watched sporting event in history. FIFA and senior officials are publicly projecting that the 2026 World Cup will reach around 6 billion viewers, or "engagements" globally, implying a substantial increase over 2022. For investors, the World Cup will provide a meaningful summer boost in revenue, engagement, and brand exposure for companies in the spotlight.

Consumer products and services 

Global sponsors and kit suppliers such as Nike (NKE), Adidas (OTCQX:ADDYY), and Puma (OTCPK:PMMAF) have cited World Cup-driven sales boosts in jersey, boot, and lifestyle apparel sales in the past two World Cups. Ticket resellers StubHub (STUB) and Vivid Seats (SEAT) are likely to see additional sales. The impact for major sponsors Airbnb (ABNB), Coca-Cola (KO), Visa (V), McDonald's (MCD), Procter & Gamble (PG), Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF), Verizon (VZ), PepsiCo's (PEP) Frito-Lay, Aramco (ARMCO), Bank of America (BAC), and Unilever (UL) is harder to predict, although there have been World Cup sponsorships that have been notable catalysts.

Sports betting and prediction markets

Expectations for sports betting around the 2026 World Cup are extremely bullish, with operators, data providers, and regulators all positioning for record volumes tied to a North American–hosted, expanded tournament. The event is widely expected to be the single biggest catalyst for global handle in the second half of the decade, especially in the U.S., where legal betting infrastructure will be far more mature than it was during the World Cup in Qatar four years ago. Hold rates for sports betting operators such as DraftKings (DKNG), FanDuel (FLUT), and BetMGM (MGM)  (OTCPK:GMVHF) could be high due to the expectation for a high mix of in-play and micro-betting during matches, which tend to have better outcomes for the house. In addition, same-game parlays are expected to be a major revenue driver, with operators set to utilize bespoke World Cup parlay products that combine player props, team outcomes, and live events, also lifting bet frequency and effective hold rates. Prediction market players Polymarket (POLYMARKET) and Kalshi (KALSHI) have event contracts for the World Cup that have already generated significant volume.

Travel and lodging

Attendance is projected above 5 million to 6.5 million in stadiums given the 48-team, 104-match format and large U.S. venues. Tourism studies estimate roughly 1.2 million incremental international visitors to the U.S. tied directly to the World Cup, with average stays around 12 days and spending north of $400 per day across the host cities. Forecasts for host metros show hotel room revenues up roughly 7–25% in June 2026 versus baseline, driven by record occupancies and rate compression, which is constructive for U.S.-exposed hotel REITs and operators. Some of the companies expected to see a boost include Marriott International (MAR), Hyatt Hotel (H), Hilton Worldwide (HLT), Booking Holdings (BKNG), Airbnb (ABNB), and Expedia (EXPE).

American Airlines (AAL), Delta Air Lines (DAL), and United Airlines (UAL) have been singled out as the airline companies most likely to see a meaningful World Cup boost.

The impact on the food and restaurant sector is harder to gauge because it can change nationally depending on which teams make a deep run. Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD), Heineken (OTCQX:HEINY), and Carlsberg (OTCPK:CABGY) have been singled out by analysts as potential World Cup winners. 

Broadcasters

Fox (FOX) and Telemundo (owned by NBCUniversal (CMCSA)) hold the U.S. television rights for the 2026 World Cup. The broadcasters are expected to see significant advertising revenue from the event being held on home soil.

ETFs in the mix 

Some ETFs that could be impacted by the World Cup economy include U.S. Global Jets ETF (JETS), Amplify Travel Tech ETF (AWAY), Harvest Travel & Leisure Index ETF (TRVL), Invesco Dynamic Leisure and Entertainment ETF (PEJ), iShares U.S. Consumer Discretionary ETF (IYC), Roundhill Sports Betting & iGaming ETF (BETZ), and the VanEck Gaming ETF (BJK).

data.title: Biotech Immatics to raise $125M through ordinary share offering
Marius Foss named CEO of Flex LNG Management
data.description:
• Flex LNG (FLNG) has named Marius Foss as the chief executive officer of Flex LNG Management AS.
  • Foss currently serves as the interim chief executive officer and chief commercial officer of Flex LNG Management AS.
  • "On behalf of the Board, I am pleased that Marius has accepted the role as CEO of Flex LNG. His proven commercial capabilities, and leadership during his tenure as interim CEO, provide a strong foundation for the company's continued development," said Mr. Ola Lorentzon, Chairman of Flex LNG.

data.title: SoftBank's Arm to bring chip training facility in South Korea
data.description:
Arm Holdings (ARM), owned by SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY), signed an MOU with South Korea's Ministry of Trade, Industry, and Resources to establish a chip design educational facility.

It is dubbed the 'Arm School,' and aims at training about 1,400 high-level semiconductor design specialists from next year until 2030, a local media report said.

This initiative, formalized during a meeting on Friday between South Korean President Lee Jae-myung and SoftBank Chairman Masayoshi Son, targets bolstering the country's semiconductor and fabless sectors, with the Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology as a primary hub.

Son highlighted AI's potential to exceed human intelligence, mentioning that Artificial Superintelligence would be "10,000 times smarter than people." He also noted that energy shortages are a key challenge for South Korea's AI ambitions.

“Through this memorandum of understanding, we have laid the foundation for nurturing key personnel who will be responsible for the future of our AI semiconductor industry,” said Kim Jung-kwan, Minister of Trade, Industry and Resources.

data.title: Euro Area GDP expands 1.4% Y/Y in Q3
data.description:
The GDP  In the Euro Area expanded 0.30% in Q3 2025 over the previous quarter, above estimates of 0.2%.

Y/Y Eurozone GDP grew 1.4%, compared to the 1.6% expansion recorded in both the first and second quarters, and broadly in line with the previous estimate. 

Spain led the major economies, expanding 2.8% (vs 3% in Q2), followed by the Netherlands at 1.6% (vs 1.7%) and France at 0.9% (up from 0.7%). 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Tesla rolls out low-cost Model 3 variant in Europe
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) on Friday launched a new lower-priced version of its Model 3 in Europe, a few months after rolling it out in the U.S., Reuters reported.

The Model 3 Standard is intended to help revive demand as Tesla faces intensifying competition from European and Chinese EV makers offering more affordable models.

Pricing for the new Model 3 Standard is set at €37,970 ($44,256) in Germany and 449,990 Swedish crowns ($47,849) in Sweden, according to the company’s website, while the model currently sells for $36,990 in the U.S.

The move follows the October launch of a lower-priced Model Y crossover in Europe.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were up 0.22% premarket at $455.32.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: HPE, RBRK, SOFI, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher in the premarket hours of Friday as investors positioned ahead of the delayed September PCE inflation report, a pivotal indicator for the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision on December 10.​

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Rubrik (RBRK) +17% - Shares jumped after the company posted a strong Q3, with revenue up 48% Y/Y and subscription revenue up 52% Y/Y. Free cash flow came in at $76.9M, sharply higher than $15.6M a year ago. For Q4, the company expects revenue of $341M–$343M, ahead of the ~$325M estimate. Rubrik also raised its FY2026 outlook, guiding revenue to $1.28B (vs. prior $1.23B and in line with estimates) and boosting its free cash flow forecast to $194M–$202M from $145M–$155M.
  • ChargePoint (CHPT) +12% – Shares rallied after the EV-charging company delivered stronger-than-expected results, with revenue up 6% Y/Y to $106M, topping the high end of its guidance range. Subscription revenue rose 15% to $42M, while GAAP gross margin improved to 31% and non-GAAP margin held at a record 33%. The company also highlighted a post-quarter debt reduction of $172M, cutting its total by more than half. For FQ4, ChargePoint guided revenue to $100M–$110M, with the $105M midpoint coming in ahead of consensus expectations of $102.89M.
  • Ulta Beauty (ULTA) +6% – Shares rose after the retailer delivered a standout Q3, posting strong beats on both revenue and earnings along with comp-store growth that nearly doubled expectations. Reflecting the robust quarter and confidence in a strong holiday season, the company raised its full-year outlook. Ulta now expects sales of roughly $12.3B, above prior guidance and ahead of the consensus of $12.06B, with comps now projected to rise 4.4%–4.7%. EPS guidance was lifted to $25.20–$25.50 from the prior $23.85 and $24.30, topping estimates of $24.57, and operating margin expectations were also raised to 12.3%–12.4% from 11.9%–12.0%.

Biggest stock losers

  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) -9% - Shares dropped sharply after the company delivered mixed fiscal Q4 results and issued guidance that fell short of several Street expectations. For FQ1, HPE projected adjusted EPS of $0.57–$0.61, topping the $0.53 consensus, but guided revenue to $9B–$9.4B, well under the anticipated $9.87B. Full-year adjusted EPS is now expected to come in at $2.25–$2.45, with the midpoint just below estimates, while free cash flow of $1.7B–$2B landed meaningfully under the $2.2B forecast.
  • SentinelOne (S) -8% – Despite the Q3 beat, shares fell as the company issued a softer-than-expected Q4 revenue outlook, guiding to $271M vs. the Street’s $271.89M estimate. FY2025 revenue is projected at $1.001B, broadly in line with expectations. The update came alongside news that CFO Barbara Larson will step down in mid-January 2026, with Chief Growth Officer Barry Padgett set to assume the interim CFO role.
  • SoFi Technologies (SOFI) -6% – Shares slipped after the company priced its public offering of 54.5M shares at $27.50, raising an expected ~$1.5B. Underwriters have a 30-day option to buy an additional ~8.2M shares. The deal is slated to close on December 8, 2025, with proceeds earmarked for general corporate purposes, strengthening capital, and supporting future growth initiatives.
  • DocuSign (DOCU) -6% - Shares slid even though the company posted Q3 results and guidance that topped expectations. For Q4, it expects revenue of $825M–$829M, with the $827M midpoint just shy of the ~$827.4M consensus. Subscription revenue is projected at $808M–$812M, and billings at $992M–$1B. Adjusted gross margin is seen at 80.8%–81.1%. The company also raised its full-year outlook, now guiding revenue to $3.208B–$3.212B, above its prior $3.19B–$3.2B range. Subscription revenue is expected at $3.14B–$3.144B, with FY billings forecast at $3.38B–$3.39B. Adjusted gross margin is now seen at 81.7%–81.8%, and adjusted operating margin at 29.8%–29.9%.
